scope_note: This panel uses Caltech’s official first-year application, academic-requirements, recommendation, and supplemental-essay pages. It is school-wide and focused on explicit Caltech guidance rather than inferred prestige signals.

areas.name: Core preparation
areas.summary: Caltech expects mastery in calculus, physics, chemistry, English, and history/social sciences before enrollment, with biology recommended.
areas.detail: Caltech says students should demonstrate mastery in four years of math including calculus, one year each of physics and chemistry, four years of English, and two years of history and/or social sciences with three or more years recommended; one year of biology is recommended.

areas.name: Substitutions when courses are unavailable
areas.summary: If calculus, chemistry, or physics is unavailable because of an unresolvable conflict, Caltech allows documented AP, IB, or Schoolhouse.world substitutes.
areas.detail: Caltech says students who cannot take calculus, chemistry, or physics because the course is unavailable or conflicts cannot be resolved may substitute specific AP 5s, IB 6/7s, or Schoolhouse.world course challenges, provided the student and counselor document the underlying issue.

areas.name: Recommendations
areas.summary: Caltech requires two teacher or instructor recommendations: one STEM and one humanities or social sciences.
areas.detail: Caltech’s first-year application checklist specifies one recommendation from calculus or beyond, chemistry, physics, or biology, and one from English, history, government, economics, or another humanities/social-science field.

areas.name: Essays and supplements
areas.summary: Caltech requires several supplemental essays and explicitly welcomes optional research or portfolio materials.
areas.detail: Caltech requires a STEM academic-interest response, one STEM experience prompt, a creativity-in-action response, and two short answers selected from four prompts. It also permits optional supplemental research papers/publications or STEM/non-STEM portfolios through the applicant portal.
